phrasal_verbs.meaning: if sth grows on you, you start to like it more
give sb or sth the same name as sb or sth else
prevent a sound from being heard by making a louder noise
believe that a trick or joke is true
try to attract people's attention and make them admire you (usually used negatively)
look or behave like an older relative
be remembered for having done something
become successful or popular very fast

part: have a part (in a play, etc)
play: have a part/role to play (in sth)

questions.sentence: Mia has a/an ...... for the lead role in the school musical next week. She's been preparing for months!
Have you read the ...... of the new Spielberg film in today's paper? The critic absolutely loved it!
I prefer to ...... documentaries on Sunday evenings rather than go out.
We really ...... ourselves at the theatre last night. The performance was absolutely outstanding!
My nephew draws a/an ...... every week for his school magazine. The other kids absolutely love it.
The drama school looks fantastic, but the annual enrolment ...... is quite steep.
The new blockbuster ...... three Oscar-winning actors and has already smashed box office records worldwide.
If you're serious about becoming a professional pianist, you need to ...... for at least two hours every single day.
The audience gasped when part of the ...... suddenly collapsed during the second act of the performance.
J.K. Rowling's first ...... about a young wizard became one of the best-selling books in publishing history.
Have you ...... that joke about the theatre director who forgot all his lines on opening night? It's hilarious!
Conducting a full symphony ...... requires years of specialist training and exceptional leadership under pressure.

B.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
B.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: My cousin, Sophie, takes (1) ...... our aunt in that they both love performing on stage.

The difference is that our aunt is a trained professional actress. Sophie, on the other hand, has had no formal training whatsoever! This doesn't stop her one bit, though — she performs at every possible opportunity and only stops when she finally drops (2) ...... exhausted at the end of a long evening.
I usually get along very well (3) ...... Sophie, but she can be genuinely embarrassing whenever she starts showing (4) ...... in front of strangers.
You can always count (5) ...... her to burst into song at the most inappropriate moment.
Last week at a dinner party, she suddenly launched into a full performance so loudly that all the other conversations in the room were completely drowned (6) ...... by her impromptu concert.
Afterwards, I told her she should apply for a spot on a TV talent show. For a moment she fell (7) ...... it completely, until I reminded her that the application deadline had passed months ago!

C.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
C.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: Freddie Mercury will go ...... in history as one of the most iconic and electrifying rock performers of all time.
The drama students are going to ...... on a production of a Shakespeare play for the end-of-year showcase.
I wasn't particularly keen on jazz when I first started listening to it, but it's really starting to ...... on me now.
Did you know that the tuxedo jacket was ...... after Tuxedo Park, an exclusive venue in New York?
That young stand-up comedian has really ...... off since her guest appearance on the late-night chat show last month.
The summer music festival is ...... round again already — we should book our tickets before they sell out!
I felt completely ...... down by the film's ending. After such a thrilling build-up, it simply fell flat.
Are you actually joking **with / about** dropping out of the drama club at this stage in the production?
Come in and sit down! **Make / Have yourself** at home and I'll get you something to drink.
After taking part **in / of** three regional singing competitions, she finally felt confident enough for the national stage.
Have **fun / funny** at the music festival this weekend — I wish I could come too!
There's nothing wrong with young children playing **with / in** musical instruments from an early age.
The audience **gave / showed** their appreciation for the cast by rising to their feet for a standing ovation.
We're thinking of **having / giving** our drama teacher a surprise farewell party before she leaves the school.
**Voicing / Speaking** your opinion as a theatre critic on a live radio programme is not as straightforward as it sounds.

E.title: Error correction (extra words)
E.instructions: Find the extra word in each line. Click it.

questions.sentence: She rushed straight [to] home after the concert to share the exciting news with her family.
It's really unkind to make the fun of someone just because they can't carry a tune.
Three remarkable new installations are currently on [the] show at the city's contemporary art gallery.
Did you find out that story funny? The whole audience was laughing from start to finish.
We had to join up the queue outside the venue more than an hour before the doors opened.
The entire cast stood in [the] silence as the director slowly read out the names of those who had won roles.
It's rare for me to laugh out loud during a film, but that comedy had me roaring [out] with laughter from beginning to end.
I will never be part with my signed programme from the original West End run of that musical.
I have the impression of that the director is seriously reconsidering the casting choices for the lead role.

F.title: Key word transformation
F.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
Did everyone have a good time at the awards ceremony?
**yourself** Did ...... the awards ceremony?

I'm certain the new film will perform well at the box office.
**bound** The new film ...... well at the box office.

Stop worrying about the stage lighting and focus on rehearsing your lines!
**instead** Focus on rehearsing your lines ...... about the stage lighting!

We arrived early enough that we didn't have to queue for tickets.
**avoid** We managed ...... there early enough.
questions.answer: avoid queuing by getting/avoid the queue by getting

questions.sentence:
Marco told us he was sorry for not inviting us to his band's debut show.
**apologised** Marco ...... us to his band's debut show.
questions.answer: apologised for not inviting/apologised for not having invited

questions.sentence:
It's fine if you return the money for the concert tickets next week.
**happy** I ...... return the money for the concert tickets next week.

Doug never usually misses a rehearsal.
**like** It ...... miss a rehearsal.

Sean is an exceptional guitarist.
**talented** Sean ...... the guitar.

You are not permitted to go backstage unless you have a crew pass.
**supposed** You ...... backstage unless you have a crew pass.

'I'll be waiting outside the theatre at nine, whatever happens,' Dan told Kyle.
**promised** Dan ...... outside the theatre at nine.

In my opinion, the best actor award should have gone to someone else.
**deserved** I don't think ...... the best actor award.

Ultimately, the opening night turned out to be a great success.
**proved** Ultimately, the opening night ...... a great success.

I can't remember which director you mentioned had won the lifetime achievement award.
**say** Which director ...... the winner of the lifetime achievement award?

G.title: Word formation (text)
G.instructions: Complete the text by changing the form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: Despite the (1) ...... (SUGGEST) from leading critics that streaming services would make traditional cinema obsolete, the big screen continues to grow steadily in (2) ...... (POPULAR).
Several major studio productions are (3) ...... (CURRENT) breaking records at cinemas worldwide, proving that film remains a form of (4) ...... (ENTERTAIN) that audiences simply cannot resist.

Indeed, many performers have become (5) ...... (FAME) through their (6) ...... (INVOLVE) in major film franchises, and have gone on to build successful careers as musicians, (7) ...... (ACT) or television hosts.
We follow with (8) ...... (EXCITE) the release of each new blockbuster, eagerly anticipating the surprises that await us on the big screen.

The (9) ...... (CONVERSE) among fans online after every new release ensures we never get (10) ...... (BORE), and the (11) ...... (VARY) mix of genres available — from action thrillers to romantic comedies — provides constant (12) ...... (AMUSE).
When a film's lead actor delivers a truly unforgettable (13) ...... (PERFORM), that only adds to the magic of the whole cinema experience.
But there is a (14) ...... (SAY) in the film industry: 'never judge a movie by its trailer', and this is especially true of big-budget productions.
